post.description:
Searches the web.

The legacy Search API reference (`/v1beta/search` endpoint) is available
[here](https://docs.parallel.ai/api-reference/legacy/search-beta/search), and
migration guide is [here](https://docs.parallel.ai/search/search-migration-guide).

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

search = client.search(
    objective="Find latest information about Parallel Web Systems. Focus on new product releases, benchmarks, or company announcements.",
    search_queries=["Parallel Web Systems products", "Parallel Web Systems announcements"],
)
print(search.results)

curl --request POST \
    --url https://api.parallel.ai/v1/search \
    --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    --header 'x-api-key: <api-key>' \
    --data '{
    "objective": "Find latest information about Parallel Web Systems. Focus on new product releases, benchmarks, or company announcements.",
    "search_queries": ["Parallel Web Systems products", "Parallel Web Systems announcements"]
}'

post.description:
Extracts relevant content from specific web URLs.

The legacy Extract API reference (`/v1beta/extract` endpoint) is available
[here](https://docs.parallel.ai/api-reference/legacy/extract-beta/extract), and
migration guide is [here](https://docs.parallel.ai/extract/extract-migration-guide).

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

extract = client.extract(
    urls=["https://www.example.com"],
    objective="Summarize the page",
)
print(extract.results)

curl --request POST \
    --url https://api.parallel.ai/v1/extract \
    --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    --header 'x-api-key: <api-key>' \
    --data '{
    "urls": ["https://www.example.com"],
    "objective": "Summarize the page"
}'

post.description: Initiates a TaskGroup to group and track multiple runs.

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

task_group = client.task_group.create(metadata={"key": "value"})
print(task_group.task_group_id)

get.description: Retrieves aggregated status across runs in a TaskGroup.

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

task_group = client.task_group.retrieve("taskgroup_id")
print(task_group.status)

get.description:
Streams events from a TaskGroup: status updates and run completions.

The connection will remain open for up to an hour as long as at least one run in the
group is still active.

schema.title: Response 200 Tasks Sessions Events Get V1 Tasks Groups  Taskgroup Id  Events Get

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

task_group_events = client.task_group.events("taskgroup_id")
for event in task_group_events:
    print(event)

post.description: Initiates multiple task runs within a TaskGroup.

x-stainless-override-schema.description: Optional header to specify the beta version(s) to enable.

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel
from parallel.types import McpServerParam
from parallel.types.run_input_param import RunInputParam

client = Parallel(api_key="API Key")
group_status = client.task_group.add_runs(
    "taskgroup_id",
    inputs=[
        RunInputParam(
            input="What was the GDP of France in 2023?",
            processor="base",
            enable_events=True,
            mcp_servers=[McpServerParam(
                type="url",
                name="parallel_web_search",
                url="https://mcp.parallel.ai/v1beta/search_mcp",
                headers={"x-api-key": "API Key"}
            )]
        )
    ]
)
print(group_status.status)

get.description:
Retrieves task runs in a TaskGroup and optionally their inputs and outputs.

All runs within a TaskGroup are returned as a stream. To get the inputs and/or
outputs back in the stream, set the corresponding `include_input` and
`include_output` parameters to `true`.

The stream is resumable using the `event_id` as the cursor. To resume a stream,
specify the `last_event_id` parameter with the `event_id` of the last event in the
stream. The stream will resume from the next event after the `last_event_id`.

schema.title: Response 200 Tasks Taskgroups Runs Get V1 Tasks Groups  Taskgroup Id  Runs Get

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

task_group_runs = client.task_group.get_runs("taskgroup_id")
for run in task_group_runs:
    print(run)

get.description:
Retrieves run status by run_id.

This endpoint is equivalent to fetching run status directly using the
`retrieve()` method or the `tasks/runs` GET endpoint.

The run result is available from the `/result` endpoint.

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

task_run = client.task_run.retrieve("run_id")
print(task_run.status)

post.description:
Initiates a task run.

Returns immediately with a run object in status 'queued'.

Beta features can be enabled by setting the 'parallel-beta' header.

x-stainless-override-schema.description: Optional header to specify the beta version(s) to enable.

402.description: Payment required: insufficient credit in account

error.message: Payment required: insufficient credit in account

429.description: Too many requests: quota temporarily exceeded

error.message: Too many requests: quota temporarily exceeded

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

task_run = client.task_run.create(
    input="What was the GDP of France in 2023?",
    processor="base",
)
print(task_run.run_id)

get.description:
Retrieves run status by run_id.

The run result is available from the `/result` endpoint.

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

task_run = client.task_run.retrieve("run_id")
print(task_run.status)

get.description:
Streams events for a task run.

Returns a stream of events showing progress updates and state changes for the task
run.

For task runs that did not have enable_events set to true during creation, the
frequency of events will be reduced.

schema.title: Response 200 Tasks Runs Events Get V1 Tasks Runs  Run Id  Events Get

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

events = client.task_run.events(run_id="run_id")
for event in events:
    print(event)

gdp.description: GDP in USD for the year, formatted like '$3.1 trillion (2023)'

get.description: Retrieves a run result by run_id, blocking until the run is completed.

x-stainless-override-schema.description: Optional header to specify the beta version(s) to enable.

404.description: Run failed or run id not found

error.message: Run failed or run id not found

x-code-samples.source:

client = Parallel()

task_run_result = client.task_run.result(run_id="run_id")
print(task_run_result.output)

get.description:
Streams events for a task run.

Returns a stream of events showing progress updates and state changes for the task

For task runs that did not have enable_events set to true during creation, the

post.description:
Return ranked entities matching a natural language objective.

This endpoint performs a best-effort search optimized for low latency. To keep
responses fast, it returns a fixed set of attributes and supports queries of
limited complexity.

For comprehensive match evaluation and enrichment, use the
[FindAll API](https://docs.parallel.ai/findall-api/findall-quickstart).

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

response = client.beta.findall.entity_search(
    entity_type="companies",
    objective="AI startups that raised Series A in 2024",
    match_limit=100,
)

for entity in response.entities:
    print(f"{entity.name}: {entity.url}")

post.description:
Transforms a natural language search objective into a structured FindAll spec.

The generated specification serves as a suggested starting point and can be further
customized by the user.

x-stainless-override-schema.description: Optional header to specify the beta version(s) to enable.

example.objective: Find all AI companies that raised Series A funding in 2024

match_conditions.name: developing_ai_products_check
match_conditions.description: Company must be developing artificial intelligence (AI) products

match_conditions.name: raised_series_a_2024_check
match_conditions.description: Company must have raised Series A funding in 2024

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

objective = "Find all portfolio companies of Khosla Ventures founded after 2020 and CEO names"

ingest = client.beta.findall.ingest(
    objective=objective,
)

print(ingest.model_dump_json(indent=2))

post.description:
Starts a FindAll run.

This endpoint immediately returns a FindAll run object with status set to 'queued'.
You can get the run result snapshot using the GET /v1beta/findall/runs/{findall_id}/result endpoint.
You can track the progress of the run by:
- Polling the status using the GET /v1beta/findall/runs/{findall_id} endpoint,
- Subscribing to real-time updates via the /v1beta/findall/runs/{findall_id}/events
endpoint,
- Or specifying a webhook with relevant event types during run creation to receive
notifications.

x-stainless-override-schema.description: Optional header to specify the beta version(s) to enable.

402.description: Payment required: insufficient credit in account

error.message: Payment required: insufficient credit in account

429.description: Too many requests: quota temporarily exceeded

error.message: Too many requests: quota temporarily exceeded

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

# Use the output from the ingest step, or provide your own values
ingest = client.beta.findall.ingest(
    objective="Find all AI companies that raised Series A funding in 2024",
)

run = client.beta.findall.create(
    objective=ingest.objective,
    entity_type=ingest.entity_type,
    match_conditions=[mc.model_dump() for mc in ingest.match_conditions],
    generator="base",
    match_limit=10,
)

print(f"FindAll run {run.findall_id} created, response:")
print(run.model_dump_json(indent=2))

x-stainless-override-schema.description: Optional header to specify the beta version(s) to enable.

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

run = client.beta.findall.retrieve(
    findall_id="findall_56ccc4d188fb41a0803a935cf485c774",
)

print(f"FindAll run {run.findall_id} status: {run.status.status}")

x-stainless-override-schema.description: Optional header to specify the beta version(s) to enable.

409.description: Cannot cancel a terminated FindAll run

error.message: Cannot cancel a terminated FindAll run

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

client.beta.findall.cancel(
    findall_id="findall_56ccc4d188fb41a0803a935cf485c774",
)

print("FindAll run cancelled.")

post.description: Add an enrichment to a FindAll run.

x-stainless-override-schema.description: Optional header to specify the beta version(s) to enable.

example.objective: Find all AI companies that raised Series A funding in 2024

match_conditions.name: developing_ai_products_check
match_conditions.description: Company must be developing artificial intelligence (AI) products

ceo_name.description: Name of the current CEO of the company. If the CEO is not publicly known, provide the name of the highest-ranking executive or founder. If no information is available, return null.

get.description:
Stream events from a FindAll run.

Args:
    request: The Shapi request
    findall_id: The FindAll run ID
    last_event_id: Optional event ID to resume from.
    timeout: Optional timeout in seconds. If None, keep connection alive
    as long as the run is going. If set, stop after specified duration.

x-stainless-override-schema.description: Optional header to specify the beta version(s) to enable.

schema.title: Response 200 Get Findall Events V1Beta Findall Runs  Findall Id  Events Get

data.name: Pika
data.description: Pika is an AI video generation platform that creates and edits videos from text prompts or images.

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

# Event types: findall.candidate.generated, findall.candidate.matched,
# findall.candidate.unmatched, findall.candidate.discarded,
# findall.candidate.enriched
events = client.beta.findall.events(
    findall_id="findall_56ccc4d188fb41a0803a935cf485c774",
)

for event in events:
    print(f"Event [{event.type}]: {event.model_dump_json()}")

post.description: Extend a FindAll run by adding additional matches to the current match limit.

x-stainless-override-schema.description: Optional header to specify the beta version(s) to enable.

example.objective: Find all AI companies that raised Series A funding in 2024

citations.title: Cognition - Devin and Cognition AI
excerpts: We're the makers of Devin, a collaborative AI teammate that helps ambitious engineering teams achieve more.
An applied AI lab building the future of software engineering

basis.reasoning: The search results repeatedly state that Cognition AI is an 'applied AI lab building the future of software engineering' and that they developed 'Devin AI', described as the 'world's first AI software engineer'. This directly confirms they are developing AI products.

excerpts: Cognition Labs Raises $21 Million Series A to Support AI Coding Products

post.description:
Create a monitor.

Monitors run on a fixed frequency to detect material changes in web content.
Set `type=event_stream` to monitor a search query, or `type=snapshot` to
monitor a specific task run's output. The monitor runs once immediately at
creation, then continues on the configured schedule.

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

monitor = client.monitor.create(
    type="event_stream",
    frequency="1d",
    settings={"query": "Extract recent news about AI"},
)
print(monitor.monitor_id)

get.description:
List monitors ordered by creation time, newest first.

Monitors are sorted by `created_at` descending. `limit` defaults to 100.
Use `next_cursor` from the response and pass it as `cursor` to fetch the
next page. Pagination ends when `next_cursor` is absent.

By default only `active` monitors are returned. Pass `status=cancelled`
or both values to include cancelled monitors.

The legacy Monitor API (`/v1alpha/monitors` endpoints) is documented under
the `Monitor (Alpha)` tag.

parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Pagination token from `next_cursor` in a previous response. Omit to start from the most recently created monitor.
schema.title: Cursor

parameters.description: Pagination token from `next_cursor` in a previous response. Omit to start from the most recently created monitor.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of monitors to return. Defaults to 100. Between 1 and 10000.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of monitors to return. Defaults to 100. Between 1 and 10000.

parameters.name: type

schema.description: Filter by monitor type. Pass multiple times to filter by multiple values. Omit to return all types.
schema.title: Type

parameters.description: Filter by monitor type. Pass multiple times to filter by multiple values. Omit to return all types.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by monitor status. Pass multiple times to filter by multiple values. Defaults to `active` only.
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by monitor status. Pass multiple times to filter by multiple values. Defaults to `active` only.

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

page = client.monitor.list(limit=10)
for monitor in page.monitors:
    print(monitor.monitor_id, monitor.status)

get.description:
Retrieve a monitor.

Retrieves a specific monitor by `monitor_id`. Returns the monitor
configuration including status, frequency, query, and webhook settings.

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

monitor = client.monitor.retrieve("monitor_id")
print(monitor.status)

post.description:
Cancel a monitor.

Permanently stops the monitor from running. Cancellation is irreversible —
create a new monitor to resume monitoring. Cancelling an already-cancelled
monitor is a no-op.

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

monitor = client.monitor.cancel("monitor_id")
print(monitor.status)

get.description:
List events for a monitor, newest first.

Pass `event_group_id` to narrow results to a single execution. Otherwise
returns all executions newest-first; use `next_cursor` to paginate.
Set `include_completions=true` to also include no-change executions.

parameters.name: event_group_id

schema.description: Filter to a single execution. Values come from `event_group_id` in webhook events and listed events. Pagination params are ignored when set.
schema.title: Event Group Id

parameters.description: Filter to a single execution. Values come from `event_group_id` in webhook events and listed events. Pagination params are ignored when set.

parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Pass `next_cursor` from a previous response to retrieve more events.
schema.title: Cursor

parameters.description: Pass `next_cursor` from a previous response to retrieve more events.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of events to return. Defaults to 20. Between 1 and 100.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of events to return. Defaults to 20. Between 1 and 100.

parameters.name: include_completions

schema.description: When true, include completion events for executions that ran but detected no material changes. Useful for auditing execution history.
schema.title: Include Completions

parameters.description: When true, include completion events for executions that ran but detected no material changes. Useful for auditing execution history.

422.description: Unprocessable content: invalid cursor or request validation error

error.message: Unprocessable content: invalid cursor or request validation error

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

page = client.monitor.events("monitor_id", limit=20)
for event in page.events:
    print(event)

post.description:
Trigger an immediate monitor run.

Enqueues a one-off execution of the monitor outside its normal schedule.
The monitor's regular schedule is not affected. An event is only emitted
if the execution detects a material change. Cancelled monitors cannot be
triggered.

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

client.monitor.trigger("monitor_id")

post.description:
Update a monitor.

Only fields explicitly included in the request body are changed. Pass
`null` for `webhook` or `metadata` to clear those fields. Pass `type` and
`settings` to update type-specific settings on an `event_stream` monitor.
At least one field must be provided. Cancelled monitors cannot be updated.

x-code-samples.source:
from parallel import Parallel

client = Parallel()

monitor = client.monitor.update(
    "monitor_id",
    frequency="12h",
    type="event_stream",
    settings={"query": "Extract recent funding news about AI startups"},
)
print(monitor.frequency)

post.description:
Chat completions.

This endpoint can be used to get realtime chat completions. It can also be used
with the Task API processors to get structured, research outputs via a chat
interface.

200.description: Returns a ChatCompletion object for non-streaming requests (application/json), or a stream of ChatCompletionResponseChunk objects for streaming requests (text/event-stream) when `stream=true` is set in the request.

fetch_policy.description: Fetch policy: determines when to return cached content from the index (faster) vs fetching live content (fresher). Default is to use a dynamic policy based on the search objective and url. Note: enabling live fetch significantly increases extract latency because it requires fetching content from source websites.

excerpt_settings.description: Controls excerpt sizes. Provide excerpt settings for fine-grained control, or omit to use defaults.

full_content.title: Full Content
full_content.description: Controls full content extraction. Set to true to enable with defaults, false to disable, or provide FullContentSettings for fine-grained control.

AdvancedExtractSettings.title: AdvancedExtractSettings
AdvancedExtractSettings.description:
Advanced extract configuration.

These settings may impact result quality and latency unless used carefully.
See https://docs.parallel.ai/search/advanced-extract-settings for more info.

source_policy.description: Domain filtering preferences: preferred and disallowed domains for monitor search results.

location.title: Location
location.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for geo-targeted monitor results.

source_policy.description: Domain and date filtering preferences for search results.

fetch_policy.description: Fetch policy: determines when to return cached content from the index (faster) vs fetching live content (fresher). Default is to disable live fetch and return cached content from the index. Note: enabling live fetch significantly increases search latency because it requires fetching content from source websites.

excerpt_settings.description: Controls excerpt sizes. Provide excerpt settings for fine-grained control, or omit to use defaults.

location.title: Location
location.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for geo-targeted search results.

max_results.title: Max Results
max_results.description: Upper bound on the number of results to return. Defaults to 10 if not provided.

AdvancedSearchSettings.title: AdvancedSearchSettings
AdvancedSearchSettings.description:
Advanced search configuration.

These settings may impact result quality and latency unless used carefully.
See https://docs.parallel.ai/search/advanced-search-settings for more info.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of schema being defined. Always `auto`.

AutoSchema.title: AutoSchema
AutoSchema.description: Auto schema for a task input or output.

id.title: Id
id.description: The id of the chat completion.

basis.title: Basis
basis.description: Basis for the chat completion, including citations and reasoning supporting the output.

interaction_id.title: Interaction Id
interaction_id.description: Identifier for this interaction. Pass as previous_interaction_id for follow-ups.

model.title: Model
model.description: The model to use for chat completions.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: The messages to use for chat completions.

stream.title: Stream
stream.description: Whether to stream the chat completions.

response_format.title: Response Format
response_format.description: The response format to use for chat completions. OpenAI compatible.

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens
max_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate. Unsupported.

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: The temperature to use for chat completions. Unsupported.

top_p.title: Top P
top_p.description: The top p to use for chat completions. Unsupported.

n.title: N
n.description: The number of chat completions to generate. Unsupported.

presence_penalty.title: Presence Penalty
presence_penalty.description: The presence penalty to use for chat completions. Unsupported.

frequency_penalty.title: Frequency Penalty
frequency_penalty.description: The frequency penalty to use for chat completions. Unsupported.

previous_interaction_id.title: Previous Interaction Id
previous_interaction_id.description: Interaction ID from a previous chat completion to use as context.

ChatCompletionRequest.title: ChatCompletionRequest
ChatCompletionRequest.description:
Request for the chat completions endpoint.

Note that all parameters except for `model`, `stream`, and `response_format`
are ignored.

role.title: Role
role.description: The role of the chat message.

content.title: Content
content.description: The content of the chat message.

name.title: Name
name.description: An optional name for the participant. Provides the model information to differentiate between participants of the same role.

ChoiceLogprobs.description: Log probability information for the choice.
ChoiceLogprobs.title: ChoiceLogprobs

Citation.description: A citation for a task output.

excerpts.description: Excerpts from the citation supporting the output. Only certain processors provide excerpts.
excerpts.title: Excerpts

Citation.title: Citation

CompletionTokensDetails.description: Breakdown of tokens used in a completion.
CompletionTokensDetails.title: CompletionTokensDetails

CompletionUsage.description: Usage statistics for the completion request.
CompletionUsage.title: CompletionUsage

type.title: Type
type.description: Type of monitor to create. `event_stream` monitors a search query for material changes; `snapshot` monitors a specific task run's output. Determines the expected shape of `settings`.

frequency.title: Frequency
frequency.description: Frequency of the monitor. Format: '<number><unit>' where unit is 'h' (hours), 'd' (days), or 'w' (weeks). Must be between 1h and 30d (inclusive).

processor.title: Processor
processor.description: Processor to use for the monitor. `lite` is faster and cheaper; `base` performs more thorough analysis at higher cost and latency. Defaults to `lite`.

webhook.description: Webhook to receive notifications about the monitor's execution.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: User-provided metadata stored with the monitor and echoed back in webhook notifications and GET responses, so you can map events to objects in your application. Keys: max 16 chars; values: max 512 chars.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Type-specific settings for the monitor. The expected shape is determined by the root `type` field: pass `MonitorEventStreamSettings` when `type` is `event_stream`, and `MonitorSnapshotSettings` when `type` is `snapshot`.

CreateMonitorRequest.title: CreateMonitorRequest
CreateMonitorRequest.description:
Request body to create a monitor.

The `type` field at the root determines the expected shape of `settings`:
`event_stream` requires `MonitorEventStreamSettings`, and `snapshot`
requires `MonitorSnapshotSettings`.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: User-provided metadata stored with the task group.

CreateTaskGroupRequest.title: CreateTaskGroupRequest
CreateTaskGroupRequest.description: Request to create a task group.

type.title: Type
type.description: Event type; always 'error'.

type.title: Type
type.description: Always 'error'.

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse
ErrorResponse.description: Response object used for non-200 status codes.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the entity to exclude from results.

url.title: Url
url.description: URL of the entity to exclude from results.

ExcludeCandidate.title: ExcludeCandidate
ExcludeCandidate.description: Exclude candidate input model for FindAll run.

content.title: Content
content.description: Content returned for http client or server errors, if any.

max_age_seconds.title: Max Age Seconds
max_age_seconds.description: Maximum age of cached content in seconds to trigger a live fetch. Minimum value 600 seconds (10 minutes).

timeout_seconds.title: Timeout Seconds
timeout_seconds.description: Timeout in seconds for fetching live content if unavailable in cache.

disable_cache_fallback.title: Disable Cache Fallback
disable_cache_fallback.description: If false, fallback to cached content older than max-age if live fetch fails or times out. If true, returns an error instead.

FetchPolicy.title: FetchPolicy
FetchPolicy.description: Policy for live fetching web results.

FieldBasis.description: Citations and reasoning supporting one field of a task output.

citations.description: List of citations supporting the output field.
citations.title: Citations

confidence.description: Confidence level for the output field. Only certain processors provide confidence levels.
confidence.title: Confidence

FieldBasis.title: FieldBasis

url.title: Url
url.description: URL that provides context or details of the entity for disambiguation.

description.title: Description
description.description: Brief description of the entity that can help answer whether entity satisfies the query.

match_status.title: Match Status
match_status.description: Status of the candidate. One of generated, matched, unmatched, discarded.

output.title: Output
output.description: Results of the match condition evaluations for this candidate. This object contains the structured output that determines whether the candidate matches the overall FindAll objective.

basis.title: Basis
basis.description: List of FieldBasis objects supporting the output.

FindAllCandidate.title: FindAllCandidate
FindAllCandidate.description:
Candidate for a find all run that may end up as a match.

Contains all the candidate's metadata and the output of the match conditions.
A candidate is a match if all match conditions are satisfied.

type.title: Type
type.description: Event type; one of findall.candidate.generated, findall.candidate.matched, findall.candidate.unmatched, findall.candidate.discarded, findall.candidate.enriched.

event_id.title: Event Id
event_id.description: Unique event identifier for the event.

data.description: The candidate whose match status has been updated.

FindAllCandidateMatchStatusEvent.title: FindAllCandidateMatchStatusEvent
FindAllCandidateMatchStatusEvent.description: Event containing a candidate whose match status has changed.

generated_candidates_count.title: Generated Candidates Count
generated_candidates_count.description: Number of candidates that were selected.

matched_candidates_count.title: Matched Candidates Count
matched_candidates_count.description: Number of candidates that evaluated to matched.

processor.title: Processor
processor.description: Processor to use for the task.

output_schema.description: JSON schema for the enrichment output schema for the FindAll run.

mcp_servers.title: Mcp Servers
mcp_servers.description: List of MCP servers to use for the task.

entity_type.title: Entity Type
entity_type.description: Type of entity to search for.

objective.title: Objective
objective.description: Natural language description of target entities.

match_limit.title: Match Limit
match_limit.description: Maximum number of entities to return. Must be between 5 and 1000 (inclusive). May return fewer results. Defaults to 100.

additional_match_limit.title: Additional Match Limit
additional_match_limit.description: Additional number of matches to find for this FindAll run. This value will be added to the current match limit to determine the new total match limit. Must be greater than 0.

status.description: Status object for the FindAll run.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Timestamp of the creation of the run, in RFC 3339 format.

modified_at.title: Modified At
modified_at.description: Timestamp of the latest modification to the FindAll run result, in RFC 3339 format.

FindAllRun.title: FindAllRun
FindAllRun.description: FindAll run object with status and metadata.

objective.title: Objective
objective.description: Natural language objective of the FindAll run.

entity_type.title: Entity Type
entity_type.description: Type of the entity for the FindAll run.

match_conditions.title: Match Conditions
match_conditions.description: List of match conditions for the FindAll run.

generator.title: Generator
generator.description: Generator for the FindAll run. One of base, core, pro, preview.

match_limit.title: Match Limit
match_limit.description: Maximum number of matches to find for this FindAll run. Must be between 5 and 1000 (inclusive). May return fewer results.

exclude_list.title: Exclude List
exclude_list.description: List of entity names/IDs to exclude from results. At most 10,000 entries are allowed.

candidates.title: Candidates
candidates.description: All evaluated candidates at the time of the snapshot.

last_event_id.title: Last Event Id
last_event_id.description: ID of the last event of the run at the time of the request. This can be used to resume streaming from the last event.

FindAllRunResult.title: FindAllRunResult
FindAllRunResult.description:
Complete FindAll search results.

Represents a snapshot of a FindAll run, including run metadata and a list of
candidate entities with their match status and details at the time the snapshot was
taken.

is_active.title: Is Active
is_active.description: Whether the FindAll run is active

metrics.description: Candidate metrics for the FindAll run.

termination_reason.title: Termination Reason
termination_reason.description: Reason for termination when FindAll run is in terminal status.

type.title: Type
type.description: Event type; always 'findall.status'.

event_id.title: Event Id
event_id.description: Unique event identifier for the event.

FindAllRunStatusEvent.title: FindAllRunStatusEvent
FindAllRunStatusEvent.description: Event containing status update for FindAll run.

objective.title: Objective
objective.description: Natural language objective of the FindAll run.
examples: Find all AI companies that raised Series A funding in 2024

entity_type.title: Entity Type
entity_type.description: Type of the entity for the FindAll run.

match_conditions.title: Match Conditions
match_conditions.description: List of match conditions for the FindAll run.

enrichments.title: Enrichments
enrichments.description: List of enrichment inputs for the FindAll run.

generator.title: Generator
generator.description: The generator of the FindAll run.

match_limit.title: Match Limit
match_limit.description: Max number of candidates to evaluate

type.title: Type
type.description: Event type; always 'findall.schema.updated'.

event_id.title: Event Id
event_id.description: Unique event identifier for the event.

FindAllSchemaUpdatedEvent.title: FindAllSchemaUpdatedEvent
FindAllSchemaUpdatedEvent.description: Event containing full snapshot of FindAll run state.

max_chars_per_result.title: Max Chars Per Result
max_chars_per_result.description: Optional limit on the number of characters to include in the full content for each url. Full content always starts at the beginning of the page and is truncated at the limit if necessary.

FullContentSettings.title: FullContentSettings
FullContentSettings.description: Optional settings for returning full content.

objective.title: Objective
objective.description: Natural language objective to create a FindAll run spec.
examples: Find all AI companies that raised Series A funding in 2024

JSONSchema.title: JSONSchema
JSONSchema.description: Structured Outputs configuration options, including a JSON Schema.

json_schema.title: Json Schema
json_schema.description: A JSON Schema object. Only a subset of JSON Schema is supported.

gdp.description: GDP in USD for the year, formatted like '$3.1 trillion (2023)'

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of schema being defined. Always `json`.

JsonSchema.title: JsonSchema
JsonSchema.description: JSON schema for a task input or output.

description.title: Description
description.description: Detailed description of the match condition. Include as much specific information as possible to help improve the quality and accuracy of Find All run results.
examples: Company must have SOC2 Type II certification (not Type I). Look for evidence in: trust centers, security/compliance pages, audit reports, or press releases specifically mentioning 'SOC2 Type II'. If no explicit SOC2 Type II mention is found, consider requirement not satisfied.

MatchCondition.title: MatchCondition
MatchCondition.description: Match condition model for FindAll ingest.

type.title: Type
type.description: Type of MCP server being configured. Always `url`.

allowed_tools.title: Allowed Tools
allowed_tools.description: List of allowed tools for the MCP server.

tool_name.title: Tool Name
tool_name.description: Name of the tool being called.

arguments.title: Arguments
arguments.description: Arguments used to call the MCP tool.

content.title: Content
content.description: Output received from the tool call, if successful.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error message if the tool call failed.

McpToolCall.title: McpToolCall
McpToolCall.description: Result of an MCP tool call.

ModerationInputModerationResult.description: A moderation result produced for the response input or output.
ModerationInputModerationResult.title: ModerationInputModerationResult

ModerationInputModerationResults.description: Successful moderation results for the request input or generated output.
ModerationInputModerationResults.title: ModerationInputModerationResults

ModerationInputModerationResultsResult.description: A moderation result produced for the response input or output.
ModerationInputModerationResultsResult.title: ModerationInputModerationResultsResult

ModerationOutputModerationResult.description: A moderation result produced for the response input or output.
ModerationOutputModerationResult.title: ModerationOutputModerationResult

ModerationOutputModerationResults.description: Successful moderation results for the request input or generated output.
ModerationOutputModerationResults.title: ModerationOutputModerationResults

ModerationOutputModerationResultsResult.description: A moderation result produced for the response input or output.
ModerationOutputModerationResultsResult.title: ModerationOutputModerationResultsResult

event_type.title: Event Type
event_type.description: Discriminant for the completion event variant.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the monitor execution completed, as an RFC 3339 string.

MonitorCompletionEvent.title: MonitorCompletionEvent
MonitorCompletionEvent.description:
Emitted when a monitor execution ran but detected no material changes.

Only returned when `include_completions=true` is passed to the list events
endpoint. Useful for auditing execution history alongside content events.

event_type.title: Event Type
event_type.description: Discriminant for the error event variant.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the monitor execution failed, as an RFC 3339 string.

MonitorErrorEvent.title: MonitorErrorEvent
MonitorErrorEvent.description:
Emitted when a monitor execution failed (e.g. payment or quota error).

Always included in the events list regardless of `include_completions`.

event_id.title: Event Id
event_id.description: Stable identifier for this event. Safe to use for client-side deduplication across pagination and retries.

event_group_id.title: Event Group Id
event_group_id.description: ID of the event group that owns this event.

event_date.title: Event Date
event_date.description: Date when this event was produced. ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) or partial (YYYY-MM or YYYY).

event_type.title: Event Type
event_type.description: Discriminant for the event_stream event variant.

output.title: Output
output.description: Text or JSON output describing the detected change.

MonitorEventStreamEvent.title: MonitorEventStreamEvent
MonitorEventStreamEvent.description:
Append-only event from an event_stream monitor.

Each event represents a distinct material change detected since the
previous execution. Events are net-new relative to the cursor; clients
should treat them as an append-only log.

output_schema.description: JSON schema that constrains and structures the event output. When set, events are returned as JSON objects matching this schema.

include_backfill.title: Include Backfill
include_backfill.description: If true, the first execution returns a sample of recent historical events matching the query (preview only — not exhaustive). If false or omitted, only events from the monitor's creation date onward are returned. Subsequent executions are always incremental.

MonitorEventStreamResponseSettings.title: MonitorEventStreamResponseSettings
MonitorEventStreamResponseSettings.description: Type-specific response fields for an `event_stream` monitor.

query.title: Query
query.description: Search query to monitor for material changes.

output_schema.description: JSON schema that constrains and structures the event output. When set, events are returned as JSON objects matching this schema instead of free-form text.

include_backfill.title: Include Backfill
include_backfill.description: If true, the first execution returns a sample of recent historical events matching the query (preview only — not exhaustive). If false or omitted, only events from the monitor's creation date onward are returned. Subsequent executions are always incremental.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of monitor.

frequency.title: Frequency
frequency.description: Frequency of the monitor. Format: '<number><unit>' where unit is 'h' (hours), 'd' (days), or 'w' (weeks). Must be between 1h and 30d (inclusive).

processor.title: Processor
processor.description: Processor to use for the monitor. `lite` is faster and cheaper; `base` performs more thorough analysis at higher cost and latency. Defaults to `lite`.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: User-provided metadata stored with the monitor and echoed back in webhook notifications and GET responses, so you can map events to objects in your application. Keys: max 16 chars; values: max 512 chars.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Timestamp of the creation of the monitor, as an RFC 3339 string.

last_run_at.title: Last Run At
last_run_at.description: Timestamp of the last run for the monitor, as an RFC 3339 string.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Type-specific configuration. Shape is determined by `type`: `MonitorEventStreamResponseSettings` for `event_stream`, `MonitorSnapshotResponseSettings` for `snapshot`.

output.description: Runtime output state. Present only for `snapshot` monitors; `null` for `event_stream` monitors.

MonitorResponse.title: MonitorResponse
MonitorResponse.description:
Response object for a monitor.

The `type` field at the root determines the concrete shape of `settings`:
`event_stream` uses `MonitorEventStreamResponseSettings`, and `snapshot`
uses `MonitorSnapshotResponseSettings`. Snapshot monitors also carry an
`output` field (`MonitorSnapshotOutput`) with the latest computed state.

event_id.title: Event Id
event_id.description: Stable identifier for this event. Safe to use for client-side deduplication across pagination and retries.

event_group_id.title: Event Group Id
event_group_id.description: ID of the event group that owns this event.

event_date.title: Event Date
event_date.description: Date when this event was produced. ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) or partial (YYYY-MM or YYYY).

event_type.title: Event Type
event_type.description: Discriminant for the snapshot event variant.

changed_output.title: Changed Output
changed_output.description: Partial output containing only the fields that changed since the previous execution, each with its `basis` (reasoning and citations).

previous_output.title: Previous Output
previous_output.description: The full output from the prior run, including all fields and basis.

MonitorSnapshotEvent.title: MonitorSnapshotEvent
MonitorSnapshotEvent.description:
Snapshot diff event emitted when a monitored task run's output changes.

`changed_output` contains only the fields that changed since the previous execution,
along with their `basis` (reasoning + citations). `previous_output` holds
the complete output from the prior run for comparison.

latest_snapshot.title: Latest Snapshot
latest_snapshot.description: Task run output from the most recent completed execution of this snapshot monitor — same structure as the output of the original task run the monitor was created from. `null` until the first run completes.

MonitorSnapshotOutput.title: MonitorSnapshotOutput
MonitorSnapshotOutput.description: Runtime output state for a `snapshot` monitor.

task_run_id.title: Task Run Id
task_run_id.description: ID of the task run used as the monitoring baseline.

query.title: Query
query.description: The original task input from the baseline task run that this monitor tracks.

output_schema.description: JSON schema derived from the baseline task run that constrains and structures the event output.

task_run_id.title: Task Run Id
task_run_id.description: Task run ID whose output becomes the data and schema for the monitor.

event_types.title: Event Types
event_types.description: Event types to send the webhook notifications for.

events.title: Events
events.description: Monitor events returned by this request, ordered newest first.

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor
next_cursor.description: Pass as `cursor` to retrieve more events. Absent when there are no more events.

warnings.title: Warnings
warnings.description: Execution caveats for this page of events, e.g. compute limits.

PaginatedMonitorEvents.title: PaginatedMonitorEvents
PaginatedMonitorEvents.description: Paginated list of monitor events, newest first.

monitors.title: Monitors
monitors.description: List of monitors for the current page.

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor
next_cursor.description: Opaque pagination token. Pass as `cursor` to retrieve the next page. Absent when there are no more pages.

PromptTokensDetails.description: Breakdown of tokens used in the prompt.
PromptTokensDetails.title: PromptTokensDetails

ResponseFormatJSONSchema.title: ResponseFormatJSONSchema
ResponseFormatJSONSchema.description:
JSON Schema response format.

Used to generate structured JSON responses.
Learn more about [Structured Outputs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

include_domains.title: Include Domains
include_domains.description: List of domains to restrict the results to. If specified, only sources from these domains will be included. Accepts plain domains (e.g., example.com, subdomain.example.gov) or bare domain extension starting with a period (e.g., .gov, .edu, .co.uk). The combined number of domains in include_domains and exclude_domains cannot exceed 200.

exclude_domains.title: Exclude Domains
exclude_domains.description: List of domains to exclude from results. If specified, sources from these domains will be excluded. Accepts plain domains (e.g., example.com, subdomain.example.gov) or bare domain extension starting with a period (e.g., .gov, .edu, .co.uk). The combined number of domains in include_domains and exclude_domains cannot exceed 200.

after_date.title: After Date
after_date.description: Optional start date for filtering search results. Results will be limited to content published on or after this date. Provided as an RFC 3339 date string (YYYY-MM-DD).

SourcePolicy.title: SourcePolicy
SourcePolicy.description:
Source policy for web search results.

This policy governs which sources are allowed/disallowed in results.

location.title: Location
location.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for geo-targeted search results.

TaskAdvancedSettings.title: TaskAdvancedSettings
TaskAdvancedSettings.description: Advanced search configuration for a task run.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: User-provided metadata stored with the group.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Timestamp of the creation of the group, as an RFC 3339 string.

TaskGroupResponse.title: TaskGroupResponse
TaskGroupResponse.description: Response object for a task group, including its status and metadata.

default_task_spec.description: Default task spec to use for the runs. If task_spec is specified in a run, it overrides this default.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: List of task runs to execute. Up to 1,000 runs can be specified per request. If you'd like to add more runs, split them across multiple TaskGroup POST requests.

TaskGroupRunRequest.title: TaskGroupRunRequest
TaskGroupRunRequest.description: Request to initiate new task runs in a task group.

run_ids.title: Run IDs
run_ids.description: IDs of the newly created runs.

run_cursor.title: Run Cursor
run_cursor.description: Cursor for these runs in the run stream at taskgroup/runs?last_event_id=<run_cursor>. Empty for the first runs in the group.

event_cursor.title: Event Cursor
event_cursor.description: Cursor for these runs in the event stream at taskgroup/events?last_event_id=<event_cursor>. Empty for the first runs in the group.

TaskGroupRunResponse.title: TaskGroupRunResponse
TaskGroupRunResponse.description: Response from adding new task runs to a task group.

num_task_runs.title: Num Task Runs
num_task_runs.description: Number of task runs in the group.

task_run_status_counts.title: Task Run Status Counts
task_run_status_counts.description: Number of task runs with each status.

is_active.title: Is Active
is_active.description: True if at least one run in the group is currently active, i.e. status is one of {'cancelling', 'queued', 'running'}.

status_message.title: Status Message
status_message.description: Human-readable status message for the group.

modified_at.title: Modified At
modified_at.description: Timestamp of the last status update to the group, as an RFC 3339 string.

type.title: Type
type.description: Event type; always 'task_group_status'.

event_id.title: Event ID
event_id.description: Cursor to resume the event stream.

TaskGroupStatusEvent.title: TaskGroupStatusEvent
TaskGroupStatusEvent.description: Event indicating an update to group status.

interaction_id.title: Interaction ID
interaction_id.description: Identifier for this interaction. Pass this value as `previous_interaction_id` to reuse context for a future request.

is_active.title: Is Active
is_active.description: Whether the run is currently active, i.e. status is one of {'cancelling', 'queued', 'running'}.

warnings.title: Warnings
warnings.description: Warnings for the run, if any.

error.description: Error for the run, present only if status is 'failed'.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: User-provided metadata stored with the run.

taskgroup_id.title: Taskgroup ID
taskgroup_id.description: ID of the taskgroup to which the run belongs.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Timestamp of the creation of the task, as an RFC 3339 string.

modified_at.title: Modified At
modified_at.description: Timestamp of the last modification to the task, as an RFC 3339 string.

type.title: Type
type.description: Event type; always 'task_run.state'.

event_id.title: Event ID
event_id.description: Cursor to resume the event stream. Always empty for non Task Group runs.

input.description: Input to the run; included only if requested.

output.title: Output
output.description: Output from the run; included only if requested and if status == `completed`.

TaskRunEvent.title: TaskRunEvent
TaskRunEvent.description:
Event when a task run transitions to a non-active status.

May indicate completion, cancellation, or failure.

processor.title: Processor
processor.description: Processor to use for the task.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: User-provided metadata stored with the run. Keys and values must be strings with a maximum length of 16 and 512 characters respectively.

source_policy.description: Optional source policy governing preferred and disallowed domains in web search results.

advanced_settings.description: Advanced search configuration for the task run.

task_spec.description: Task specification. If unspecified, defaults to auto output schema.

input.title: Input
input.description: Input to the task, either text or a JSON object.
examples: What was the GDP of France in 2023?

previous_interaction_id.title: Previous Interaction Id
previous_interaction_id.description: Interaction ID to use as context for this request.

mcp_servers.title: Mcp Servers
mcp_servers.description: Optional list of MCP servers to use for the run.

enable_events.title: Enable Events
enable_events.description: Controls tracking of task run execution progress. When set to true, progress events are recorded and can be accessed via the [Task Run events](https://docs.parallel.ai/api-reference) endpoint. When false, no progress events are tracked. Note that progress tracking cannot be enabled after a run has been created. The flag is set to true by default for premium processors (pro and above).

webhook.description:
Callback URL (webhook endpoint) that will receive an HTTP POST when the run completes. 
This feature is not available via the Python SDK.

basis.title: Basis
basis.description: Basis for each top-level field in the JSON output. Per-list-element basis entries are available only when the `parallel-beta: field-basis-2025-11-25` header is supplied.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of output being returned, as determined by the output schema of the task spec.

mcp_tool_calls.title: Mcp Tool Calls
mcp_tool_calls.description: MCP tool calls made by the task.

beta_fields.title: Beta Fields
beta_fields.description: Deprecated. mcp-server-2025-07-17 is now included directly in the output (e.g. mcp_tool_calls).

content.title: Content
content.description: Output from the task as a native JSON object, as determined by the output schema of the task spec.

output_schema.title: Output Schema
output_schema.description: Output schema for the Task Run. Populated only if the task was executed with an auto schema.

TaskRunJsonOutput.title: TaskRunJsonOutput
TaskRunJsonOutput.description: Output from a task that returns JSON.

type.title: Type
type.description: Event type; always starts with 'task_run.progress_msg'.

TaskRunProgressMessageEvent.title: TaskRunProgressMessageEvent
TaskRunProgressMessageEvent.description: A message for a task run progress update.

type.title: Type
type.description: Event type; always 'task_run.progress_stats'.

source_stats.description: Source stats describing progress so far.

progress_meter.title: Progress Meter
progress_meter.description: Completion percentage of the task run. Ranges from 0 to 100 where 0 indicates no progress and 100 indicates completion.

TaskRunProgressStatsEvent.title: TaskRunProgressStatsEvent
TaskRunProgressStatsEvent.description: A progress update for a task run.

output.title: Output
output.description: Output from the task conforming to the output schema.

num_sources_considered.title: Num Sources Considered
num_sources_considered.description: Number of sources considered in processing the task.

num_sources_read.title: Num Sources Read
num_sources_read.description: Number of sources read in processing the task.

sources_read_sample.title: Sources Read Sample
sources_read_sample.description: A sample of URLs of sources read in processing the task.

TaskRunSourceStats.title: TaskRunSourceStats
TaskRunSourceStats.description: Source stats for a task run.

basis.title: Basis
basis.description: Basis for the output. The basis has a single field 'output'.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of output being returned, as determined by the output schema of the task spec.

mcp_tool_calls.title: Mcp Tool Calls
mcp_tool_calls.description: MCP tool calls made by the task.

beta_fields.title: Beta Fields
beta_fields.description: Deprecated. mcp-server-2025-07-17 is now included directly in the output (e.g. mcp_tool_calls).

TaskRunTextOutput.title: TaskRunTextOutput
TaskRunTextOutput.description: Output from a task that returns text.

output_schema.title: Output Schema
output_schema.description: JSON schema or text fully describing the desired output from the task. Descriptions of output fields will determine the form and content of the response. A bare string is equivalent to a text schema with the same description.

input_schema.title: Input Schema
input_schema.description: Optional JSON schema or text description of expected input to the task. A bare string is equivalent to a text schema with the same description.

TaskSpec.title: TaskSpec
TaskSpec.description:
Specification for a task.

Auto output schemas can be specified by setting `output_schema={"type":"auto"}`. Not
specifying a TaskSpec is the same as setting an auto output schema.

For convenience bare strings are also accepted as input or output schemas.

description.title: Description
description.description: A text description of the desired output from the task.
examples: GDP in USD for the year, formatted like '$3.1 trillion (2023)'

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of schema being defined. Always `text`.

TextSchema.title: TextSchema
TextSchema.description: Text description for a task input or output.

query.title: Query
query.description: Updated search query for the monitor. Use this for minor updates to prompts and instructions only. Major changes to the query may lead to unexpected results in change detection, as the monitor compares new results with what was previously seen.

UpdateMonitorEventStreamSettings.title: UpdateMonitorEventStreamSettings
UpdateMonitorEventStreamSettings.description: Type-specific update settings for an `event_stream` monitor.

type.title: Type
type.description: Type of the monitor being updated. Required when `settings` is provided; must be `event_stream` (snapshot monitors have no updatable type-specific settings).

frequency.title: Frequency
frequency.description: Frequency of the monitor. Format: '<number><unit>' where unit is 'h' (hours), 'd' (days), or 'w' (weeks). Must be between 1h and 30d (inclusive).

webhook.description: Webhook to receive notifications about the monitor's execution.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: User-provided metadata stored with the monitor and echoed back in webhook notifications and GET responses, so you can map events to objects in your application. Keys: max 16 chars; values: max 512 chars.

settings.description: Type-specific settings to update. Only valid when `type` is `event_stream`. Pass `settings.query` to update the prompt, or `null` for `settings.advanced_settings` to clear it.

UpdateMonitorRequest.title: UpdateMonitorRequest
UpdateMonitorRequest.description:
Request body to update a monitor.

Only fields that are explicitly included in the request body are updated.
Pass `null` for `webhook` or `metadata` to clear those fields. To update
type-specific settings on an `event_stream` monitor, include `type` and
`settings`; pass `settings.query` to update the prompt, or `null` for
`settings.advanced_settings` to clear it. If `settings` is provided, `type`
is required to identify the settings shape. The request must still include
at least one field to update; empty updates fail validation.

UsageItem.title: UsageItem
UsageItem.description: Usage item for a single operation.

max_chars_per_result.title: Max Chars Per Result
max_chars_per_result.description: Optional upper bound on the total number of characters to include per url. Excerpts may contain fewer characters than this limit to maximize relevance and token efficiency.

V1ExcerptSettings.title: V1ExcerptSettings
V1ExcerptSettings.description: Optional settings for returning relevant excerpts.

urls.title: Urls
urls.description: URLs to extract content from. Up to 20 URLs.

objective.title: Objective
objective.description: As in SearchRequest, a natural-language description of the underlying question or goal driving the request. Used together with search_queries to focus excerpts on the most relevant content.

search_queries.title: Search Queries
search_queries.description: Optional keyword search queries, as in SearchRequest. Used together with objective to focus excerpts on the most relevant content.

max_chars_total.title: Max Chars Total
max_chars_total.description: Upper bound on total characters across excerpts from all extracted results.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Session identifier to track calls across separate search and extract calls, to be used as part of a larger task. Specifying it may give better contextual results for subsequent API calls.

client_model.title: Client Model
client_model.description: The model generating this request and consuming the results. Enables optimizations and tailors default settings for the model's capabilities.

advanced_settings.description: Advanced configuration for fetch policy, excerpt settings, and full content settings. May impact result quality and latency unless used carefully. When omitted, excerpts are enabled and full content is disabled by default.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Extract errors: requested URLs not in the results.

warnings.title: Warnings
warnings.description: Warnings for the extract request, if any.

usage.title: Usage
usage.description: Usage metrics for the extract request.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Session identifier. Echoed back from the request if provided, otherwise generated by the server. Should be passed to future search and extract calls made by the agent as part of the same larger task.

url.title: Url
url.description: URL associated with the search result.

title.title: Title
title.description: Title of the webpage, if available.

publish_date.title: Publish Date
publish_date.description: Publish date of the webpage in YYYY-MM-DD format, if available.

excerpts.title: Excerpts
excerpts.description: Relevant excerpted content from the URL, formatted as markdown.

full_content.title: Full Content
full_content.description: Full content from the URL formatted as markdown, if requested.

V1ExtractResult.title: V1ExtractResult
V1ExtractResult.description: Extract result for a single URL.

objective.title: Objective
objective.description: Natural-language description of the underlying question or goal driving the search. Used together with search_queries to focus results on the most relevant content. Should be self-contained with enough context to understand the intent of the search.

search_queries.title: Search Queries
search_queries.description: Concise keyword search queries, 3-6 words each. At least one query is required, provide 2-3 for best results. Used together with objective to focus results on the most relevant content.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Search mode preset: supported values are `turbo`, `basic`, and `advanced`. Turbo mode is optimized for the fastest responses. Basic mode offers low latency and works best with 2-3 high-quality search_queries. Advanced mode provides higher quality with more advanced retrieval and compression. Defaults to `advanced` when omitted.

max_chars_total.title: Max Chars Total
max_chars_total.description: Upper bound on total characters across excerpts from all results.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Session identifier to track calls across separate search and extract calls, to be used as part of a larger task. Specifying it may give better contextual results for subsequent API calls.

client_model.title: Client Model
client_model.description: The model generating this request and consuming the results. Enables optimizations and tailors default settings for the model's capabilities.

advanced_settings.description: Advanced configuration for source policy, fetch policy, and excerpt settings. May impact result quality and latency unless used carefully. When omitted, excerpts are enabled by default.

results.title: Results
results.description: A list of search results, ordered by decreasing relevance.

warnings.title: Warnings
warnings.description: Warnings for the search request, if any.

usage.title: Usage
usage.description: Usage metrics for the search request.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Session identifier, echoed back from the request if provided, otherwise generated by the server. Should be passed to future search and extract calls made by the agent as part of the same larger task.

url.title: Url
url.description: URL associated with the search result.

title.title: Title
title.description: Title of the webpage, if available.

publish_date.title: Publish Date
publish_date.description: Publish date of the webpage in YYYY-MM-DD format, if available.

excerpts.title: Excerpts
excerpts.description: Relevant excerpted content from the URL, formatted as markdown.

V1WebSearchResult.title: V1WebSearchResult
V1WebSearchResult.description: A single search result from the web search API.

type.title: Type
type.description: Type of warning. Note that adding new warning types is considered a backward-compatible change.

event_types.title: Event Types
event_types.description: Event types to send the webhook notifications for.

openai__types__chat__chat_completion__Mo.title: Moderation
openai__types__chat__chat_completion__Mo.description:
Moderation results for the request input and generated output, if moderated
completions were requested.

openai__types__chat__chat_completion__Mo.title: ModerationInputError
openai__types__chat__chat_completion__Mo.description: An error produced while attempting moderation.

openai__types__chat__chat_completion__Mo.title: ModerationOutputError
openai__types__chat__chat_completion__Mo.description: An error produced while attempting moderation.

openai__types__shared_params__response_f.title: ResponseFormatJSONObject
openai__types__shared_params__response_f.description:
JSON object response format.

An older method of generating JSON responses.
Using `json_schema` is recommended for models that support it. Note that the
model will not generate JSON without a system or user message instructing it
to do so.

openai__types__shared_params__response_f.title: ResponseFormatText
openai__types__shared_params__response_f.description: Default response format. Used to generate text responses.

ChoiceDelta.description: A chat completion delta generated by streamed model responses.
ChoiceDelta.title: ChoiceDelta

ChoiceDeltaFunctionCall.description:
Deprecated and replaced by `tool_calls`.

The name and arguments of a function that should be called, as generated by the model.
ChoiceDeltaFunctionCall.title: ChoiceDeltaFunctionCall

Moderation.description: Moderation results for the response input and output, if moderated completions were requested.
Moderation.title: Moderation

ModerationInputError.title: ModerationInputError

ModerationOutputError.description: An error produced while attempting moderation for the response input or output.
ModerationOutputError.title: ModerationOutputError

type.description: The type of the chat completion chunk. Always `chat.completion.chunk`.
type.title: Type

id.description: The id of the chat completion response chunk.
id.title: Id

basis.description: Basis for the chat completion chunk, including citations and reasoning supporting the output.
basis.title: Basis

interaction_id.description: Identifier for this interaction. Pass as previous_interaction_id for follow-ups.
interaction_id.title: Interaction Id

tags.name: Search
tags.description:
Search returns ranked URLs with extended excerpts suitable for LLM consumption. Inputs are a natural-language objective and optional keyword queries. Source policies allow including or excluding specific domains and have configurable output sizes. The returned extended snippets contain dense, relevant information from relevant pages.
- Result: ranked list with URL, title, and long text excerpts

tags.name: Extract
tags.description:
Extract returns excerpts or full content from one or more URLs. Inputs are a list of URLs and an optional search objective and keyword queries. The returned excerpts or full content is formatted as markdown and suitable for LLM consumption.
- Result: excerpts or full content from the URL formatted as markdown

tags.name: Tasks
tags.description:
The Task API executes web research and extraction tasks. Clients submit a natural-language objective with an optional input schema; the service plans retrieval, fetches relevant URLs, and returns outputs that conform to a provided or inferred JSON schema. Supports deep research style queries and can return rich structured JSON outputs. Processors trade-off between cost, latency, and quality. Each processor supports calibrated confidences.
- Output metadata: citations, excerpts, reasoning, and confidence per field

Task Groups enable batch execution of many independent Task runs with group-level monitoring and failure handling.
- Submit hundreds or thousands of Tasks as a single group
- Observe group progress and receive results as they complete
- Real-time updates via Server-Sent Events (SSE)
- Add tasks to an existing group while it is running
- Group-level retry and error aggregation

tags.name: FindAll
tags.description: The FindAll API discovers and evaluates entities that match complex criteria from natural language objectives. Submit a high-level goal and the service automatically generates structured match conditions, discovers relevant candidates, and evaluates each against the criteria. Returns comprehensive results with detailed reasoning, citations, and confidence scores for each match decision. Streaming events and webhooks are supported.

tags.name: Monitor
tags.description:
The Monitor API watches the web for material changes on a fixed frequency. Each monitor runs once on creation and then on its configured schedule, emitting events when meaningful changes are detected.
- `event_stream` monitors track a search query and emit an event for each new material change.
- `snapshot` monitors track a specific task run's output and emit an event when the output changes.

Results can be polled via the events endpoint or delivered via webhooks.

tags.name: Chat API (Beta)
tags.description: The Chat API provides a programmatic chat-style text generation interface. It accepts a sequence of messages and returns model responses. Intended for assistant-like interactions and evaluation. Streaming responses are supported.
